## Objective
<!-- What is this change trying to achieve? State the purpose clearly and specifically. -->

---

## Scope
<!-- List the exact files and paths affected by this PR. No change outside this scope is included. -->

- [ ] Scope is confined to what was agreed. No unrelated changes are included.

---

## Evidence collected
<!-- Per evidence-first.md: decisions must be based on observable repo state, not assumptions. -->

- [ ] Current git status reviewed before changes were made
- [ ] All referenced paths confirmed to exist
- [ ] Diffs reviewed — no unintended modifications included
- [ ] Validation performed (lint / test / run) where applicable

Validation notes:
<!-- Describe what was run and what was observed. -->

---

## Human gate confirmation
<!-- Per human-gate-rules.md: any git commit or push requires explicit human approval. -->

- [ ] Scope confirmed by human reviewer
- [ ] Exact files affected have been reviewed
- [ ] Expected outcomes are understood
- [ ] Rollback strategy is understood

Reviewer:
<!-- Name or handle of the authorizing human. -->

---

## Risk and rollback
<!-- Per baseline-rollback-v1.0.md: a named and restorable baseline must exist before promotion. -->

Current baseline reference:
<!-- Commit SHA, tag, or equivalent identifier of the last stable state. -->

Rollback plan:
<!-- How to revert if this change introduces breakage. Must be executable from the baseline reference alone. -->

Known risks:
<!-- Any conditions where this change could cause runtime errors, broken interfaces, or UX degradation. -->

---

## Change type

- [ ] Documentation improvement (direct PR permitted per CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [ ] RFC proposal (must reference `/rfcs` entry)
- [ ] Governance protocol change (requires RFC)
- [ ] Feature / demo / runtime change
- [ ] Other: <!-- describe -->

---
